concerns.text: Bootstrap resampling 'at dataset level' with n=1000 is statistically invalid when only ≥5 datasets exist. You cannot meaningfully resample 5 items 1000 times. Bootstrap should be at the observation level within datasets, not at the dataset level.

Linear mixed-effects model with random slopes for base rate and class imbalance on only 5 datasets is overparameterized. Random slopes require sufficient groups (typically ≥10-20) to estimate variance components reliably. This will not converge or will produce unstable estimates.

Correlation computed 'within-dataset (across models)' ignores hierarchical nesting. Metrics are nested within models within datasets. Treating all metric pairs as independent violates correlation assumptions and inflates Type I error.

Construct validity issue: 'base rate difference' is undefined. Base rate is typically a single value per dataset (proportion of positive outcomes). What is being compared to create a 'difference'? This must be clarified before regression can be specified.

With only 5 datasets, regression power is severely limited. The research.md acknowledges this ('findings are exploratory') but the methodology does not adjust (e.g., no power analysis, no alternative approach for small-N). This undermines the ability to detect effects.
